{{Japanese Clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀の位置について』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Japanese Clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Japanese Clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Japanese Clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Japanese Clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能}} The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能}} {{Japanese Clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能}} {{Japanese Clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能}} {{Japanese Clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Usa clan}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能}} {{Japanese Clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能}} {{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名= 宇佐氏 |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= 宇佐公（君）後の宇佐宿祢を本姓とする宮成・到津・岩根・安心院・出光の5大家 |家祖= [[菟狭津彦命]] |種別= [[社家]]
[[華族]]（[[男爵]]）[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト]から。[[到津公誼]]が男爵となった。 |出身地= [[豊前国]][[宇佐郡]]？ |根拠地= [[宇佐八幡宮]]付近 |人物= |支流=[[佐知氏]](佐知翁)
[[秋吉氏]](佐知翁の孫24代宇佐維広三男) }} '''宇佐氏'''（うさうじ）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]の一つ。古代には'''[[宇佐国造]]'''や宇佐[[評督]]を務め、後に'''[[宇佐八幡宮]]'''の[[社家]]として繁栄した。 == 概要 == 宇佐氏は『[[記紀]]』にみえる[[神武天皇|神武朝]]の[[菟狭津彦命]]（うさつひこのみこと）を家祖とし、[[宇佐国造]]を世襲したとされる。菟狭津彦命の父は、[[高皇産霊尊]]の子の[[天活玉命]]の子である天三降命とされ、天三降命は『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天神本紀」に豊国宇佐国造等の祖として登場し、同書神代系紀にも[[神世七代]]の一柱として登場する。 [[奈良時代]]に[[八幡神]]に[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]の権力が及ぶと、宇佐八幡宮にも神宮司が設置された『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』、141 - 142ページ。当初は大宮司職は[[和人]]の[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]]（おおが）が、[[祭祀]]は渡来系氏族の[[辛嶋氏]]（からしま）が掌握していたが、[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|道鏡事件]]が起こると大宮司職が空席になった為[[宇佐池守]]が任じられるところとなった『八幡宮創祀の位置について』。 そもそも[[奈良時代]]までは宇佐にあっても[[八幡宮]]であって、[[比咩神]]（ひめがみ）を信仰する宇佐氏の「[[宇佐宮]]」ではなく、宇佐氏が八幡宮に関係するのは[[天平勝宝]]元年と同7年(755)より[[宝亀]]4年(773)の18年という限られた時代であった。 宇佐宮最古の文書『[[八幡宇佐宮御託宣集]]』には「今坐宮号菱形小椋山也、比咩神大御神前住、国加都玉依比咩命也、又住都麻垣比咩大御神也、本坐宇佐郡安心別倉東方高岳也、」とあり、宇佐神宮に比咩神が関わるのは、比咩神が鎮座した山に八幡神が移住したためである。 [[平安時代]]では、大神氏が大宮司、宇佐氏が少宮司と定められたが、[[石清水八幡宮]]の勧請の後大宮司職が空席になった隙に宇佐氏が宮司となった。八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 平安時代末期には[[宇佐公則]]が現れ、[[平氏]]と結んでその社領は[[九州]]全域に及んだ。[[宇佐公道]]は清盛の娘を娶り平氏との繋がりが深かったので、壇ノ浦前に自宅を安徳帝の仮御所にして迎え入れた。[[鎌倉時代]]も宇佐氏による大宮司独占が続き、[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]には[[宇佐公敦]]、次いでその弟の[[宇佐公連]]が大宮司となったが、公連は[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]に属して独立し、南北朝の争いが宇佐八幡宮にも及んだ。 [[向野郷]]宮成山から'''宮成氏'''を称し、公連は[[到津]]へ赴いて'''到津氏'''を称し。[[宮崎別府]]'''岩根氏'''を称し。[[安心院]]'''安心院氏'''を称した。それぞれ大宮司となり、宇佐一族間で激しく争った。のちは平氏から源氏に代わるなかで源氏と結びつきが強い宮成家、到津家が大宮司として残ることになる。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]は[[大内氏]]・[[大友氏]]などに仕えたものの、宇佐氏の[[武士]]としての活動は目立たない。また、同時代には[[奈多鑑基]]によって所領が蝕まれ、大友軍によって宇佐宮を焼き討ちされて一族が宇佐から避難する事件も起きている。 [[黒田氏]]が[[豊前国]]へ入国してくると[[宮成公里]]の子・[[宮成公基]]は[[宮成公尚]]に大宮司を譲り、[[黒田長政]]に仕えて武士となった。 公尚以降は宮成・到津両家が続き、近代に至った。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』- [[安部猛]] *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀の位置について』 - [[中野幡能]] {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うさうし}} [[Category:宇佐氏|!]] [[Category:宇佐神宮|氏うさうし]] [[Category:社家の氏族]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] [[Category:豊前国の氏族]] [[Category:大分県の歴史]]

{{日本の氏族 |家名= 宇佐氏 |家紋= |家紋名称= |本姓= 宇佐公（君）後の宇佐宿祢を本姓とする宮成・到津・岩根・安心院・出光の5大家 |家祖= [[菟狭津彦命]] |種別= [[社家]]
[[華族]]（[[男爵]]）[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト]から。[[到津公誼]]が男爵となった。 |出身地= [[豊前国]][[宇佐郡]]？ |根拠地= [[宇佐八幡宮]]付近 |人物= |支流=[[佐知氏]](佐知翁)
[[秋吉氏]](佐知翁の孫24代宇佐維広三男) }} '''宇佐氏'''（うさうじ）は、[[日本]]の[[氏族]]の一つ。古代には'''[[宇佐国造]]'''や宇佐[[評督]]を務め、後に'''[[宇佐八幡宮]]'''の[[社家]]として繁栄した。 == 概要 == 宇佐氏は『[[記紀]]』にみえる[[神武天皇|神武朝]]の[[菟狭津彦命]]（うさつひこのみこと）を家祖とし、[[宇佐国造]]を世襲したとされる。菟狭津彦命の父は、[[高皇産霊尊]]の子の[[天活玉命]]の子である天三降命とされ、天三降命は『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「天神本紀」に豊国宇佐国造等の祖として登場し、同書神代系紀にも[[神世七代]]の一柱として登場する。 [[奈良時代]]に[[八幡神]]に[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]の権力が及ぶと、宇佐八幡宮にも神宮司が設置された『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』、141 - 142ページ。当初は大宮司職は[[和人]]の[[大神氏 (豊後国)|大神氏]]（おおが）が、[[祭祀]]は渡来系氏族の[[辛嶋氏]]（からしま）が掌握していたが、[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件|道鏡事件]]が起こると大宮司職が空席になった為[[宇佐池守]]が任じられるところとなった『八幡宮創祀の位置について』。 そもそも[[奈良時代]]までは宇佐にあっても[[八幡宮]]であって、[[比咩神]]（ひめがみ）を信仰する宇佐氏の「[[宇佐宮]]」ではなく、宇佐氏が八幡宮に関係するのは[[天平勝宝]]元年と同7年(755)より[[宝亀]]4年(773)の18年という限られた時代であった。 宇佐宮最古の文書『[[八幡宇佐宮御託宣集]]』には「今坐宮号菱形小椋山也、比咩神大御神前住、国加都玉依比咩命也、又住都麻垣比咩大御神也、本坐宇佐郡安心別倉東方高岳也、」とあり、宇佐神宮に比咩神が関わるのは、比咩神が鎮座した山に八幡神が移住したためである。 [[平安時代]]では、大神氏が大宮司、宇佐氏が少宮司と定められたが、[[石清水八幡宮]]の勧請の後大宮司職が空席になった隙に宇佐氏が宮司となった。八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 平安時代末期には[[宇佐公則]]が現れ、[[平氏]]と結んでその社領は[[九州]]全域に及んだ。[[宇佐公道]]は清盛の娘を娶り平氏との繋がりが深かったので、壇ノ浦前に自宅を安徳帝の仮御所にして迎え入れた。[[鎌倉時代]]も宇佐氏による大宮司独占が続き、[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]には[[宇佐公敦]]、次いでその弟の[[宇佐公連]]が大宮司となったが、公連は[[南朝 (日本)|南朝]]に属して独立し、南北朝の争いが宇佐八幡宮にも及んだ。 [[向野郷]]宮成山から'''宮成氏'''を称し、公連は[[到津]]へ赴いて'''到津氏'''を称し。[[宮崎別府]]'''岩根氏'''を称し。[[安心院]]'''安心院氏'''を称した。それぞれ大宮司となり、宇佐一族間で激しく争った。のちは平氏から源氏に代わるなかで源氏と結びつきが強い宮成家、到津家が大宮司として残ることになる。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]は[[大内氏]]・[[大友氏]]などに仕えたものの、宇佐氏の[[武士]]としての活動は目立たない。また、同時代には[[奈多鑑基]]によって所領が蝕まれ、大友軍によって宇佐宮を焼き討ちされて一族が宇佐から避難する事件も起きている。 [[黒田氏]]が[[豊前国]]へ入国してくると[[宮成公里]]の子・[[宮成公基]]は[[宮成公尚]]に大宮司を譲り、[[黒田長政]]に仕えて武士となった。 公尚以降は宮成・到津両家が続き、近代に至った。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』- [[安部猛]] *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀の位置について』 - [[中野幡能]] {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うさうし}} [[Category:宇佐氏|!]] [[Category:宇佐神宮|氏うさうし]] [[Category:社家の氏族]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] [[Category:豊前国の氏族]] [[Category:大分県の歴史]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Normdaten}} {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[en:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]]

However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[en:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | classification = {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kazoku|ja|華族|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = {{ill|Buzen Province|ja|豊前国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Usa District|ja|宇佐郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese {{ill|clan|ja|氏族|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:japanese clans]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[en:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]]

In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, as detailed in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}, when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the {{ill|Miwa clan|ja|大神氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa clan|qq=}} held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the {{ill|Tempyo Shoho|ja|天平勝宝|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tempyo Shoho|qq=}} era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across {{ill|Kyushu|ja|九州|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu|qq=}}. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Usa clan served lords like the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōtomo Clan|ja|大友氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Clan|qq=}}. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the {{ill|Kuroda Clan|ja|黒田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Clan|qq=}} took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagamasa|qq=}}. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[en:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]]
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]][[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District]]? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sachi Clan|qq=}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[en:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]]

{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[en:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:宇佐氏]] [[Category:宇佐神宮]]
