{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[en:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:宇佐氏]] [[Category:宇佐神宮]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:豊前国の氏族]]

In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:宇佐氏]] [[Category:宇佐神宮]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:豊前国の氏族]]

[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]][[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District]]? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sachi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 評}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛島氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:九州地方の氏族]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:宇佐氏]] [[Category:宇佐神宮]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]]
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]][[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District]]? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sachi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 評}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛島氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22128876]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]]
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]][[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District]]? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sachi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 評}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛島氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]]

During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=|1=Hatayoshi Nakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367576}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Usa clan]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q22128876]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]]
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]][[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District]]? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sachi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 評|1=hyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631303}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛島氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=|1=Hatayoshi Nakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367576}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Usa clan]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q22128876]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]]
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]][[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District]]? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sachi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 評|1=hyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631303}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛島氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=|1=Hatayoshi Nakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367576}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[en:Usa clan]]

{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q22128876]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]]
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]][[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District]]? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sachi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 評|1=hyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631303}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛島氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=|1=Hatayoshi Nakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367576}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Usa clan]][[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Usa clan]] [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[sn:sa clan]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] {{wikidata link|Q22128876}}

Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=|1=Hatayoshi Nakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367576}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{wikidata link|Q22128876}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q22128876]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]]
[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]][[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District]]? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sachi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 評|1=hyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631303}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛島氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=|1=Hatayoshi Nakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367576}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] {{wikidata link|Q22128876}}

[[ja:宇佐氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q22128876]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Usa Clan | crest_image = | crest_name = | original_name = Usa no Kimi, later Usa no Sukune as the family name of the five prominent families: Miyase, Itozu, Iwane, Ajimu, and Idemitsu | founder = {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}} | classification = [[:en:Shake (social class)|Shrine family]]

Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛島氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|qq=|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|qq=|1=Hatayoshi Nakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367576}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] {{wikidata link|Q22128876}}

[[:en:Kazoku|Kazoku]] (Barons)[https://reichsarchiv.jp/%E5%9F%BA%E7%A4%8E%E3%83%87%E3%83%BC%E3%82%BF/%E6%97%A5%E6%9C%AC%E8%8F%AF%E6%97%8F%E3%80%801011%E5%AE%B6%E3%83%AA%E3%82%B9%E3%83%88 Reichsarchiv華族リスト] - Itozu Kiyoshi was ennobled as a baron. | origin = [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]][[:en:Usa District, Ōita|Usa District]]? | stronghold = Near {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮|1=Usa Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q715632}} | notable_members = | branches = {{ill|Sachi Clan|ja|佐知氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sachi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O)
{{ill|Akiyoshi Clan|ja|秋吉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akiyoshi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (descendants of Sachi-O’s grandson, Usa Korehiro, 24th generation) }} '''Usa Clan''' (宇佐氏) is a Japanese [[:en:Clan|clan]]. In ancient times, the clan served as {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and Usa {{ill|Hyotoku|ja|評督|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyotoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 評|1=hyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11631303}}, later prospering as the shrine family of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}. == Overview == The Usa clan traces its lineage back to {{ill|Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, a figure mentioned in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, as the ancestral founder and hereditary {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Usatsuhiko no Mikoto was said to be the son of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}, as detailed in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]. During the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], when the imperial court extended influence over the worship of Hachiman, a shrine administrator was appointed to {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇佐神宮}}.『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』, pp. 141-142 Initially, the [[:en:Miwa clan|Miwa clan]] held the position of Great Shrine Administrator (Ōmiya no Sakan), while the {{ill|Karashima Clan|ja|辛嶋氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karashima Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 辛島氏}}, a clan of immigrant origin, oversaw rituals. However, during the {{ill|Dōkyō Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō Incident|qq=}}, Usa Ikemori assumed the Great Shrine Administrator role when the position became vacant.『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 Until the Nara period, the site was primarily dedicated to Hachiman worship, unrelated to the Usa clan’s original faith in the deity {{ill|Himegami|ja|比咩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}. The clan’s association with Hachiman only began in limited periods between 755 and 773, during the [[:en:Tenpyō-shōhō|Tempyo Shoho]] era. According to the oldest records, such as the {{ill|Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|ja|八幡宇佐宮御託宣集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Usa Shrine Oracle Collection|qq=}}, Himegami was originally enshrined on a mountain later occupied by Hachiman. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the Ōgami clan held the role of Great Shrine Administrator, while the Usa clan served as Lesser Shrine Administrators. However, after the founding of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]], the Usa clan took advantage of the vacancy to become Shrine Administrators.八幡宇佐宮御託宣集 By the late Heian period, figures like {{ill|Usa Kinnori|ja|宇佐公則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kinnori|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} emerged, extending the clan’s influence across [[:en:Kyushu|Kyushu]]. Usa Kimichi married a daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, hosting the exiled Emperor Antoku in his residence during the {{ill|Battle of Dan-no-ura|ja|壇ノ浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Dan-no-ura|1=Dan-no-ura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10931453}}. During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the clan retained control of Usa Hachiman Shrine. Throughout the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Usa clan served lords like the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]] and [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Clan]]. However, their military role diminished, and events like the burning of Usa Shrine led to the clan’s displacement. After the [[:en:Kuroda clan|Kuroda Clan]] took control of Buzen Province, Usa Kōri’s descendants, such as Miyase Kōki, transitioned into vassals of [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. == Notes == === Annotations === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist}} == References == *『戦国人名事典 コンパクト版』 - {{ill|Abe Takeshi|ja|安部猛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Takeshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} *『末広八幡宮社記』 *『秋吉系図』 *『八幡宮創祀のlocationについて』 - {{ill|Nakanohata Nō|ja|中野幡能|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakanohata Nō|1=Hatayoshi Nakano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11367576}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|宇佐氏|version=100442650|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[ja:宇佐氏]] [[Category:Clans of Kyūshū Region]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Usa Jingū]] [[Category:Shake clans]] [[Category:Clans of Buzen Province]] [[Category:Shinbetsu]][[Category:Shinbetsu]] {{wikidata link|Q22128876}}
